title: Homebrew-based Fleet-maintained apps input schema
description: Specify metadata to source a Fleet-maintained app from Homebrew.

name.description: The user-facing name of the application.

unique_identifier.description: The platform-specific unique identifier for the app. On macOS, this is the app's bundle identifier.

token.description: The identifier used by homebrew for the app; it is the token field on the homebrew API response.

installer_format.description: The file format for the app's installer.

slug.description: The slug identifies a specific app and platform combination. It is used to name the manifest files that contain the metadata that Fleet needs to add, install, and uninstall this app. Format: app-name/platform (e.g., adobe-acrobat-reader/darwin)

pre_uninstall_scripts.description: Command lines that will be run before the generated uninstall script is executed.

post_uninstall_scripts.description: Command lines that will be run after the generated uninstall script is executed.

default_categories.description: The default categories assigned to the installer in self-service if no categories are specified when it is added to a team's library.

install_script_path.description: A filepath to an install script. If provided, this script will override the generated install script.

uninstall_script_path.description: A filepath to an uninstall script. If provided, this script will override the generated uninstall script.

frozen.description: If true, the app will not be processed during ingestion and no new output will be created.

cask_path.description: Path (relative to the repo root) to a local file containing the cask JSON in the same schema as https://formulae.brew.sh/api/cask/<token>.json. Used to commit cask metadata for third-party taps directly into this repo under inputs/homebrew/custom-tap/.

signature.description: Pinned signing identity for the app's installer, verified at ingest (cmd/maintained-apps/verify) and in the validator before the install script runs. Pin the Developer ID Team ID (it survives certificate renewals); an identity change fails verification and requires a reviewed pin update.

apple_team_id.description: The Apple Developer ID Team ID expected to have signed the installer (e.g. "M683GB7CPW").

notarized.description: Whether the installer is expected to carry an Apple notarization ticket.

unsigned.description: Acknowledges that the vendor ships an unsigned installer. Requires a justification and is mutually exclusive with the identity pins.

justification.description: Why an unsigned installer is acceptable for this app. Required when unsigned is true.
